description: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) tricks an authenticated victim's browser into sending unintended state-changing requests to a web application. Because the browser automatically includes session cookies, the application cannot distinguish the forged request from a legitimate one unless CSRF tokens or SameSite cookie policies are enforced.

mitre_attack.mapping_note: Enterprise ATT&CK has no direct CSRF technique. T1185 is related only when the operation hijacks or manipulates an authenticated browser session; it must not be presented as a one-to-one CSRF mapping.

cvss_methodology.note: CVSS measures vulnerability severity. It does not prescribe SOC response deadlines.
cvss_methodology.migration_note: CVSS v4.0 is current, but its metrics and scoring are not one-to-one with v3.1. Existing scenario vectors remain v3.1 until individually re-assessed.

response_framework.name: NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 3
response_framework.note: Response events implement Detect, Respond, Recover, and continuous-improvement outcomes. Numeric exercise thresholds are local policy, not NIST requirements.

timing_model.note: The 900-second floor prevents zero-length and division-by-zero windows at CVSS 10.0. These values are simulation policy and must be calibrated with organizational risk and service objectives.

incident_response_rules.description: Alert triage started within the difficulty-adjusted MTTA threshold
incident_response_rules.trigger: alert_investigation_started occurs after mtta_threshold_sec or is absent

incident_response_rules.description: Incident assessed and confirmed or formally dismissed
incident_response_rules.trigger: neither incident_confirmed nor dismissal_approved is present

incident_response_rules.description: Relevant evidence preserved before destructive containment
incident_response_rules.trigger: incident_confirmed is present but evidence_preserved is absent or occurs after containment_initiated
containment_initiated is absent or occurs after ttc_max_sec

incident_response_rules.description: Root cause or malicious artifact eradicated after containment
incident_response_rules.trigger: containment_succeeded is present but eradication_completed is absent or out of order

incident_response_rules.description: Recovery validated before normal operation resumed
incident_response_rules.trigger: eradication_completed is present but recovery_validated is absent or out of order

incident_response_rules.description: Lessons learned and improvement actions recorded
incident_response_rules.trigger: recovery_validated is present but lessons_learned_recorded is absent or out of order

incident_response_rules.description: Medium-or-higher alerts are not denied without documented approval
incident_response_rules.trigger: alert_denied is present for severity medium or higher without a preceding dismissal_approved event

scoring.ttc_factor: 1 when containment succeeds by ttc_expected; linear decay to 0 at ttc_max; 0 when containment is absent or later than ttc_max

response_difficulty_bonus.condition: awarded only when containment_succeeded is present
response_difficulty_bonus.zero_delay_policy: A zero-second investigation delay uses a one-second denominator and therefore receives the capped bonus.

custom_rules.description: State-changing request has an authenticated cookie, lacks a valid anti-CSRF token, and Origin/Referer is absent or untrusted
Rule 100501 condition plus a credentialed cross-origin response or reflected untrusted Origin
CSP frame-ancestors report is correlated with an authenticated privileged state-changing request

wazuh.note: Custom entries are dataset detection specifications and must be implemented and tested in the deployed Wazuh manager before use. Stock rule descriptions and levels match the linked upstream ruleset.

scenarios.label: Forged Funds Transfer via CSRF — No Confidentiality Impact, High Integrity
scenarios.description: Attacker hosts a malicious page that silently triggers a POST /transfer request using the victim's authenticated banking session. No CSRF token present on the endpoint. Confidentiality unaffected; integrity severely impacted by unauthorised fund transfer. Scope unchanged.

attack_context.rationale: The attacker themselves requires no credentials; the attack exploits the victim's authenticated session.

component_rationale.AV_N: Attack delivered remotely via attacker-controlled web page
component_rationale.AC_L: No special conditions; endpoint lacks CSRF token entirely
component_rationale.PR_N: Attacker does not need an account; exploits victim's existing session
component_rationale.UI_R: Victim must visit the attacker-controlled page
component_rationale.S_U: Impact confined to the victim's account actions; no lateral scope change
component_rationale.C_N: No data is read or disclosed to the attacker
component_rationale.I_H: Unauthorised financial transaction executed with high integrity impact

detection.rationale: Detection uses custom Wazuh rule 100501 (State-changing request lacks an anti-CSRF token and has a foreign origin); the rule definition and required telemetry are documented in this attack record.

wazuh_alert.description: State-changing request lacks an anti-CSRF token and has a foreign origin

event_log.detail: Wazuh custom rule 100501 level 8 fired: State-changing request lacks an anti-CSRF token and has a foreign origin.
Analyst-1 opened alert — MTTA 600s, within 750s medium threshold. Cross-referenced Referer with transfer logs; session cookie matched authenticated user victim@example.com.

Analyst-2 confirmed CSRF: transfer request originated from external page via auto-submitted form; X-CSRF-Token absent from request; legitimate transfer page always includes it. Fraudulent transfer of $5000 confirmed.
Relevant logs, request data, volatile evidence, and indicators were preserved with chain-of-custody metadata.
Analyst-3 invalidated victim session; transaction reversal initiated with payment processor; CSRF token validation enforced on all state-changing endpoints via middleware patch.

CSRF token middleware active; repeat attack attempt returns 403 CSRF validation failed. Session invalidated and reissued. SameSite=Strict applied to session cookie. TTC_actual=9500s < TTC_expected=12600s.
Malicious artifacts and the vulnerable execution path were removed or disabled; root cause remediation was recorded.
Service health, security controls, and monitoring were validated before normal operation resumed.
Post-incident review captured root cause, response gaps, owners, and tracked improvement actions.

scenarios.label: CSRF with Partial Confidentiality — MTTA Breach and Alert Dismissed
scenarios.description: Attacker forges a POST request that both executes a state change and returns user PII in the response body (confidentiality L). MTTA exceeded and the Wazuh alert dismissed without incident confirmation, leaving the attacker undetected.

attack_context.rationale: Authentication requirement follows the endpoint access described by the scenario.

component_rationale.AV_N: Attack delivered remotely via attacker-controlled page
component_rationale.AC_L: Endpoint lacks CSRF token; no special conditions
component_rationale.UI_R: Victim must visit the attacker-controlled page
component_rationale.S_U: Impact confined to victim account scope
component_rationale.C_L: Response body leaks limited PII (account number masked) to attacker via CORS misconfiguration
component_rationale.I_H: Unauthorised state change with high integrity impact

detection.rationale: CORS misconfiguration combined with CSRF is a subtle compound vulnerability; Referer header checks alone are insufficient as the forged request mimics same-origin patterns via iframe nesting. Wazuh correlation is less reliable at this confidence level. Detection uses custom Wazuh rule 100502 (Cross-origin state change combined with unsafe CORS response); the rule definition and required telemetry are documented in this attack record.

wazuh_alert.description: Cross-origin state change combined with unsafe CORS response

event_log.detail: Wazuh custom rule 100502 level 8 fired: Cross-origin state change combined with unsafe CORS response.
Analyst-1 opened alert — MTTA 1100s, exceeds 900s high threshold. CSRF-R01 triggered. Cross-origin POST noted; analyst assessed as legitimate cross-origin API call from partner domain.
The analyst denied the alert without documented approval. This triggers CSRF-R09; all other missing response actions are evaluated independently.

scenarios.label: Nested Iframe CSRF with Clickjacking — Scope Changed, Good Response
scenarios.description: Attacker uses a clickjacked iframe nested inside a legitimate-looking page to trigger a CSRF request. The CORS misconfiguration allows partial response read-back. Scope changed because the forged request crosses a subdomain boundary, affecting a sibling application service. High attack complexity due to specific iframe nesting requirements.

attack_context.rationale: Authentication requirement follows the endpoint access described by the scenario.

component_rationale.AC_H: Nested iframe and clickjacking overlay required; specific same-site cookie configuration must be absent
component_rationale.UI_R: Victim must click within the attacker's clickjacking overlay
component_rationale.S_C: Forged request crosses to sibling subdomain service (sub2.example.com) outside the primary web app scope
component_rationale.C_L: Limited PII disclosed via CORS read-back from sibling service
component_rationale.I_H: High integrity impact: privileged action executed on sibling service

detection.rationale: CSP violation reports from the browser and X-Frame-Options bypass alerts in Wazuh provide reasonable detection signal; however correlating the CSRF attempt with the clickjacking overlay requires cross-application log correlation. Detection uses custom Wazuh rule 100503 (Framing violation correlated with a privileged state-changing request); the rule definition and required telemetry are documented in this attack record.

wazuh_alert.description: Framing violation correlated with a privileged state-changing request

event_log.detail: Wazuh custom rule 100503 level 8 fired: Framing violation correlated with a privileged state-changing request.
Analyst-1 opened alert — MTTA 700s, within 750s medium threshold. CSP violation log showed iframe embedding from attacker domain; Wazuh correlated with POST to sibling admin endpoint.
Analyst-2 confirmed attack chain: clickjacked iframe triggered admin action on sub2 service; CORS misconfiguration on sub2 returned partial account data to attacker origin. Admin action reversed.
Relevant logs, request data, volatile evidence, and indicators were preserved with chain-of-custody metadata.
Analyst-3 added X-Frame-Options: DENY and Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'none' to sub2 service; CSRF tokens enforced on all admin endpoints; SameSite=Strict on admin session cookie.

Re-test confirms iframe embedding blocked (CSP violation with no execution); CSRF token now required on admin actions. CORS policy restricted to known partner origins only. TTC_actual=9800s < TTC_expected=11160s.
Malicious artifacts and the vulnerable execution path were removed or disabled; root cause remediation was recorded.
Service health, security controls, and monitoring were validated before normal operation resumed.
Post-incident review captured root cause, response gaps, owners, and tracked improvement actions.

references.title: FIRST CVSS v4.0 Specification (current migration target)

dataset_metadata.source_policy: Use upstream standards and vendor rulesets; label project-specific detection logic as custom.
dataset_metadata.cvss_profile: Validated CVSS v3.1 compatibility profile; migrate through a separate expert review rather than automatic vector conversion.
